projects.primaryBeneficiary: Affordable and mid-income homebuyers in stalled housing projects
projects.storyBlurb: Launched in November 2019, SWAMIH Fund 1.0 provides last-mile financing to stalled affordable and mid-income housing projects across India. Before its launch, around 1,509 stalled projects involving approximately 4.58 lakh units required ~₹55,000 crore to complete. As of 15 December 2025, approximately 61,000 homes have been delivered across 110 projects, including over 7,000 units under rehabilitation.

projects.developer: Government of India / Ministry of Finance
projects.notes: Announced in Budget 2025-26, SWAMIH Fund 2 builds on the success of SWAMIH Fund 1.0 with a ₹15,000 crore corpus aimed at completing an additional 1 lakh stalled housing units across India. It continues the government's strategy of priority
projects.primaryBeneficiary: Affordable and mid-income homebuyers in stalled housing projects
projects.storyBlurb: Announced in Budget 2025-26, SWAMIH Fund 2 builds on the success of SWAMIH Fund 1.0 with a ₹15,000 crore corpus aimed at completing an additional 1 lakh stalled housing units across India. It continues the government's strategy of priority last-mile financing to revive stranded residential projects in the affordable and mid-income segments.

projects.developer: Government of India / Asian Development Bank / Government of Tamil Nadu
projects.projectName: Inclusive, Resilient and Sustainable Housing for Urban Poor Sector Project (Tamil Nadu)
projects.notes: On 7 December 2021, the Government of India and the Asian Development Bank signed a $150 million loan to provide inclusive, resilient, and sustainable housing for the urban poor in Tamil Nadu. The project targets improved access to dignifie
projects.storyBlurb: On 7 December 2021, the Government of India and the Asian Development Bank signed a $150 million loan to provide inclusive, resilient, and sustainable housing for the urban poor in Tamil Nadu. The project targets improved access to dignified and disaster-resilient homes for low-income urban residents in the state.
